acceptance_criteria: Default policy round-trips to JSON identically to §9.6.
prd_refs: §9.12 — these defaults form the safety floor that user/project config cannot weaken.
description: Produce the default `policy.json` — safe write roots, protected roots, `neverFastExecute` list, `fastAllowedCommands` list, blocked / risky regex patterns, blocked-behavior toggles, and confirmation tokens — exactly as in PRD §9.6.
notes: Regex strings embed double escaping in JSON (`\\b`); ensure tests pull the parsed string and feed it directly to `[regex]::new`.
Do not modify regex patterns — they are part of the safety contract documented in PRD.
steps: TDD: Default policy contains all sections required by §9.6.
TDD: `policy.protectedRoots` includes both `~` and `~/.ssh` plus the cross-platform list.
TDD: All `blockedRegexPatterns` compile as valid .NET regex.
TDD: All `riskyRegexPatterns` compile as valid .NET regex.
Pester tests verify presence of each list, regex compilation, and default token values.
name: [cfg-03] Default policy (policy.json) generator
